name: Binary Search
description: Efficiently finds a target value in a sorted array by repeatedly dividing the search interval in half.
pseudocode:
function binarySearch(arr, target):
  low = 0, high = len(arr) - 1
  while low <= high:
    mid = (low + high) / 2
    if arr[mid] == target: return mid
    else if arr[mid] < target: low = mid + 1
    else: high = mid - 1
  return -1

name: Merge Sort
description: A divide-and-conquer algorithm that splits the array in half, recursively sorts each half, then merges the sorted halves.

name: Quick Sort
complexity: O(n log n) avg, O(n²) worst
description: Picks a pivot element, partitions the array around it so smaller elements go left and larger go right, then recurses.

name: Depth-First Search (DFS)
description: Explores a graph by going as deep as possible along each branch before backtracking. Uses a stack (or recursion).

name: Breadth-First Search (BFS)
description: Explores a graph level by level, visiting all neighbors before moving deeper. Uses a queue.

name: Dijkstra's Algorithm
description: Finds the shortest path from a source node to all other nodes in a weighted graph with non-negative edges.

name: Dynamic Programming (Fibonacci)
description: Computes Fibonacci numbers efficiently by storing previously computed results to avoid redundant work.

name: Kadane's Algorithm
description: Finds the contiguous subarray with the maximum sum in a one-dimensional array.
pseudocode:
function maxSubarraySum(arr):
  maxSoFar = arr[0]
  maxEndingHere = arr[0]
  for i = 1 to len(arr) - 1:
    maxEndingHere = max(arr[i],
                       maxEndingHere + arr[i])
    maxSoFar = max(maxSoFar, maxEndingHere)
  return maxSoFar

name: Knapsack (0/1)
description: Determines the maximum value that can be carried in a knapsack of capacity W, given n items each with a weight and value.

name: Two Pointers
description: Uses two pointers moving towards each other (or in the same direction) to solve problems on sorted arrays or linked lists.

name: Sliding Window
description: Maintains a window over a sequence that slides forward, efficiently computing properties like max sum of k consecutive elements.
pseudocode:
function maxSumWindow(arr, k):
  windowSum = sum(arr[0..k-1])
  maxSum = windowSum
  for i = k to len(arr) - 1:
    windowSum += arr[i] - arr[i - k]
    maxSum = max(maxSum, windowSum)
  return maxSum

name: Topological Sort
description: Orders vertices of a directed acyclic graph (DAG) so that for every edge u→v, u comes before v.

name: Union-Find (Disjoint Set)
description: Tracks a set of elements partitioned into disjoint subsets. Supports near-constant time union and find with path compression and rank.

name: Binary Search Tree (BST) Insert
description: Inserts a value into a BST by traversing left for smaller values and right for larger values until finding an empty spot.

name: Heap Sort
description: Builds a max-heap from the array, then repeatedly extracts the maximum element to build the sorted result.

name: A* Search
description: A best-first pathfinding algorithm that uses a heuristic to estimate the cost to the goal, combining actual cost g(n) and heuristic h(n).

name: LRU Cache
description: A cache that evicts the least recently used item when full. Implemented with a hash map and a doubly linked list.

name: Trie (Prefix Tree)
complexity: O(m) per operation, m = word length
description: A tree-like data structure for efficient retrieval of keys in a dataset of strings. Each node represents a character.

name: Floyd-Warshall
description: Finds shortest paths between all pairs of vertices in a weighted graph, including negative edges (but no negative cycles).

name: Counting Sort
description: A non-comparison sort that counts occurrences of each value and uses arithmetic to place elements in the correct position.
